county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Harris County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Harrisburg). County seat is Houston. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Houston. Original Texas county (Harrisburg municipality) — largest county in Texas by population. Houston Metropolitan Research Center at Houston Public Library is the major repository. Harris County Archives. San Jacinto Battlefield (1836). Port of Houston records. Massive immigrant communities (German, Czech, Vietnamese, Mexican, African American). Harris County Deed Records online from 1836. Houston Academy of Medicine/Texas Medical Center Library.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Harris County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Houston holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Harris County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Harris County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1836. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Harris County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Harris County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Harris County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Harris County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Harris County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Harris County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Houston
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Harris County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Harris County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Harrison County formed 1839 from Shelby. County seat is Marshall. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Marshall. East Texas county — second-largest slaveholding county in Texas before the Civil War. Marshall was briefly the Confederate capital of Missouri. Harrison County Historical Museum. Wiley College (historically Black college). Significant African American and plantation records. Check Shelby for pre-1839 records. Caddo Lake area heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Harrison County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1839; County Clerk in Marshall holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Harrison County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hartley County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Channing. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Channing. Northwest Panhandle county organized 1891. XIT Ranch territory (three million acres). XIT Museum in Dalhart (Dallam County). Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Very sparse early population.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Hartley County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Channing holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Haskell County formed 1858 from Fannin, Milam. County seat is Haskell. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Haskell. Rolling Plains county organized 1885. Haskell County Historical and Genealogical Society. Farming (cotton) and ranching. Check Fannin and Milam for pre-organization records. Indian frontier era.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Haskell County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Haskell holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Haskell County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Haskell County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Haskell County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Haskell County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Haskell County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Haskell County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hays County formed 1848 from Travis. County seat is San Marcos. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in San Marcos. Hill Country county between Austin and San Antonio. Hays County Historical Commission. Texas State University archives in San Marcos. Aquarena Springs/San Marcos Springs area. Check Travis for pre-1848 records. German and Mexican heritage. LBJ's early political career here.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Hays County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in San Marcos holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Hays County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Hays County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Hays County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Hays County from 1848. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hemphill County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Canadian. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Canadian. Northeast Panhandle county organized 1887. River Valley Pioneer Museum. Canadian River area ranching. Hemphill County Historical Association. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Hemphill County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Canadian holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Henderson County formed 1846 from Houston, Nacogdoches. County seat is Athens. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Athens. East Texas county. Henderson County Historical Commission. Athens pottery heritage. Check Houston and Nacogdoches for pre-1846 records. Significant African American community. Cedar Creek Reservoir area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Henderson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Athens holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hidalgo County formed 1852 from Cameron, Starr. County seat is Edinburg. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Edinburg. Rio Grande Valley county — the most populous of the Valley counties. Museum of South Texas History in Edinburg. Mexican land grant heritage. Significant Mexican-American records and heritage. Citrus industry records. Check Cameron and Starr for pre-1852 records. Border crossing records at NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Hidalgo County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1852; County Clerk in Edinburg holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1852 to 1950 for Hidalgo County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Hidalgo County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Hidalgo County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Hidalgo County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Hidalgo County from 1852. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1874 and 1993 courthouse fires; 1993 fire destroyed some records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hill County formed 1853 from Navarro. County seat is Hillsboro. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1874 and 1993 courthouse fires; 1993 fire destroyed some records.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hillsboro. 1874 and 1993 courthouse fires — 1993 fire destroyed some records. Central Texas county. Hill County Cell Block Museum. Cotton farming heritage. Check Navarro for pre-1853 records. Confederate Cotton Bureau records. Czech and German communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Hill County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1853; County Clerk in Hillsboro holds deed books
